articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Substitute Nishimura strikes to earn Japan draw against Uruguay
article.description: Japan secured a 1-1 draw with Uruguay in Tokyo on Friday as substitute Takuma Nishimura scored with his first touch.
article.linkText: Sub Nishimura earns Japan draw with Uruguay

images.name: Japan celeb vs. Uruguay_20230324 [1296x729]
images.caption: Takuma Nishimura #19 of Japan celebrates scoring his team's first goal during the international friendly match between Japan and Uruguay at the National Stadium on March 24, 2023 in Tokyo, Japan.

peers.caption: Takuma Nishimura #19 of Japan celebrates scoring his team's first goal during the international friendly match between Japan and Uruguay at the National Stadium on March 24, 2023 in Tokyo, Japan.

article.story:
Japan secured a 1-1 draw with Uruguay in Tokyo on Friday as substitute Takuma Nishimura scored with his first touch to cancel out Federico Valverde's opener for the visitors in the Samurai Blue's first outing since last year's World Cup.

The Yokohama F Marinos man pounced on Junya Ito's inviting low centre from the right 15 minutes from time after Valverde had given Uruguay a 38th minute lead.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Valverde's goal had come against the run of play, the Real Madrid midfielder reacting quickest after his initial shot had come back off the post and, with goalkeeper Daniel Schmidt on the floor, the Uruguayan headed into the empty net.

The game was Japan's first since coach Hajime Moriyasu extended his contract after leading the country into the knockout rounds at the World Cup in Qatar and the starting line-up featured a mixture of old and new for the home side.

Right back Yukinari Sugawara was making only his second appearance for Japan, more than two-and-a-half years after his debut, while previously uncapped Ayumu Seko partnered Ko Itakura in the centre of a new-look defence.

But it Japan's second half substitutes who earned Moriyasu's side a deserved draw.

Ito, on as a replacement for Ritsu Doan in the 61st minute, saw a penalty initially awarded by referee Ko Hyun-jin overturned by the Video Assistant Referee before the Reimes winger teed up Nishimura to score the equaliser.

The Uruguayans, who will face South Korea in Seoul on Tuesday, almost snatched the win five minutes from time when Fecundo Torres hit the crossbar.
